translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The proverbs of Solomon, the son of David, king of Israel:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to know wisdom and instruction; to discern the words of understanding;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to receive instruction in wise dealing, in righteousness, justice, and equity;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to give prudence to the simple, knowledge and discretion to the young man:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that the wise man may hear, and increase in learning; that the man of understanding may attain to sound counsel:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to understand a proverb, and parables, the words and riddles of the wise.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The fear of Yahweh  is the beginning of knowledge; but the foolish despise wisdom and instruction.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My son, listen to your father’s instruction, and don’t forsake your mother’s teaching:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for they will be a garland to grace your head, and chains around your neck.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My son, if sinners entice you, don’t consent.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If they say, “Come with us. Let’s lay in wait for blood. Let’s lurk secretly for the innocent without cause.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let’s swallow them up alive like Sheol,  and whole, like those who go down into the pit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: We’ll find all valuable wealth. We’ll fill our houses with plunder.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall cast your lot among us. We’ll all have one purse.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My son, don’t walk on the path with them. Keep your foot from their path,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for their feet run to evil. They hurry to shed blood.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For in vain is the net spread in the sight of any bird:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but these lay wait for their own blood. They lurk secretly for their own lives.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So are the ways of everyone who is greedy for gain. It takes away the life of its owners.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Wisdom calls aloud in the street. She utters her voice in the public squares.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She calls at the head of noisy places. At the entrance of the city gates, she utters her words:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “How long, you simple ones, will you love simplicity? How long will mockers delight themselves in mockery, and fools hate knowledge?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Turn at my reproof. Behold,  I will pour out my spirit on you. I will make known my words to you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Because I have called, and you have refused; I have stretched out my hand, and no one has paid attention;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but you have ignored all my counsel, and wanted none of my reproof;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I also will laugh at your disaster. I will mock when calamity overtakes you;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: when calamity overtakes you like a storm, when your disaster comes on like a whirlwind; when distress and anguish come on you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then will they call on me, but I will not answer. They will seek me diligently, but they will not find me;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: because they hated knowledge, and didn’t choose the fear of Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They wanted none of my counsel. They despised all my reproof.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore they will eat of the fruit of their own way, and be filled with their own schemes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the backsliding of the simple will kill them. The careless ease of fools will destroy them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But whoever listens to me will dwell securely, and will be at ease, without fear of harm.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My son, if you will receive my words, and store up my commandments within you;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So as to turn your ear to wisdom, and apply your heart to understanding;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, if you call out for discernment, and lift up your voice for understanding;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you seek her as silver, and search for her as for hidden treasures:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then you will understand the fear of Yahweh, and find the knowledge of God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For Yahweh gives wisdom. Out of his mouth comes knowledge and understanding.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He lays up sound wisdom for the upright. He is a shield to those who walk in integrity;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that he may guard the paths of justice, and preserve the way of his saints.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then you will understand righteousness and justice, equity and every good path.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For wisdom will enter into your heart. Knowledge will be pleasant to your soul.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Discretion will watch over you. Understanding will keep you,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to deliver you from the way of evil, from the men who speak perverse things;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who forsake the paths of uprightness, to walk in the ways of darkness;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who rejoice to do evil, and delight in the perverseness of evil;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who are crooked in their ways, and wayward in their paths:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: To deliver you from the strange woman, even from the foreigner who flatters with her words;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who forsakes the friend of her youth, and forgets the covenant of her God:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for her house leads down to death, her paths to the departed spirits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: None who go to her return again, neither do they attain to the paths of life:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that you may walk in the way of good men, and keep the paths of the righteous.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the upright will dwell in the land. The perfect will remain in it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the wicked will be cut off from the land. The treacherous will be rooted out of it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My son, don’t forget my teaching; but let your heart keep my commandments:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for length of days, and years of life, and peace, will they add to you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t let kindness and truth forsake you. Bind them around your neck. Write them on the tablet of your heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So you will find favor, and good understanding in the sight of God and man.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Trust in Yahweh with all your heart, and don’t lean on your own understanding.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In all your ways acknowledge him, and he will make your paths straight.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t be wise in your own eyes. Fear Yahweh, and depart from evil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It will be health to your body, and nourishment to your bones.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Honor Yahweh with your substance, with the first fruits of all your increase:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: so your barns will be filled with plenty, and your vats will overflow with new wine.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My son, don’t despise Yahweh’s discipline, neither be weary of his reproof:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for whom Yahweh loves, he reproves; even as a father reproves the son in whom he delights.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Happy is the man who finds wisdom, the man who gets understanding.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For her good profit is better than getting silver, and her return is better than fine gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She is more precious than rubies. None of the things you can desire are to be compared to her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Length of days is in her right hand. In her left hand are riches and honor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Her ways are ways of pleasantness. All her paths are peace.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She is a tree of life to those who lay hold of her. Happy is everyone who retains her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: By wisdom Yahweh founded the earth. By understanding, he established the heavens.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: By his knowledge, the depths were broken up, and the skies drop down the dew.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My son, let them not depart from your eyes. Keep sound wisdom and discretion:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: so they will be life to your soul, and grace for your neck.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then you shall walk in your way securely. Your foot won’t stumble.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When you lie down, you will not be afraid. Yes, you will lie down, and your sleep will be sweet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t be afraid of sudden fear, neither of the desolation of the wicked, when it comes:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for Yahweh will be your confidence, and will keep your foot from being taken.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t withhold good from those to whom it is due, when it is in the power of your hand to do it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t say to your neighbor, “Go, and come again; tomorrow I will give it to you,” when you have it by you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t devise evil against your neighbor, since he dwells securely by you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t strive with a man without cause, if he has done you no harm.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t envy the man of violence. Choose none of his ways.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the perverse is an abomination to Yahweh, but his friendship is with the upright.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s curse is in the house of the wicked, but he blesses the habitation of the righteous.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Surely he mocks the mockers, but he gives grace to the humble.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The wise will inherit glory, but shame will be the promotion of fools.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Listen, sons, to a father’s instruction. Pay attention and know understanding;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for I give you sound learning. Don’t forsake my law.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For I was a son to my father, tender and an only child in the sight of my mother.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He taught me, and said to me: “Let your heart retain my words. Keep my commandments, and live.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Get wisdom. Get understanding. Don’t forget, neither swerve from the words of my mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t forsake her, and she will preserve you. Love her, and she will keep you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Wisdom is supreme. Get wisdom. Yes, though it costs all your possessions, get understanding.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Esteem her, and she will exalt you. She will bring you to honor, when you embrace her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She will give to your head a garland of grace. She will deliver a crown of splendor to you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Listen, my son, and receive my sayings. The years of your life will be many.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have taught you in the way of wisdom. I have led you in straight paths.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When you go, your steps will not be hampered. When you run, you will not stumble.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Take firm hold of instruction. Don’t let her go. Keep her, for she is your life.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t enter into the path of the wicked. Don’t walk in the way of evil men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Avoid it, and don’t pass by it. Turn from it, and pass on.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For they don’t sleep, unless they do evil. Their sleep is taken away, unless they make someone fall.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For they eat the bread of wickedness, and drink the wine of violence.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the path of the righteous is like the dawning light, that shines more and more until the perfect day.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The way of the wicked is like darkness. They don’t know what they stumble over.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My son, attend to my words. Turn your ear to my sayings.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let them not depart from your eyes. Keep them in the center of your heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For they are life to those who find them, and health to their whole body.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Keep your heart with all diligence, for out of it is the wellspring of life.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Put away from yourself a perverse mouth. Put corrupt lips far from you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let your eyes look straight ahead. Fix your gaze directly before you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Make the path of your feet level. Let all of your ways be established.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t turn to the right hand nor to the left. Remove your foot from evil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My son, pay attention to my wisdom. Turn your ear to my understanding:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that you may maintain discretion, that your lips may preserve knowledge.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the lips of an adulteress drip honey. Her mouth is smoother than oil,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but in the end she is as bitter as wormwood, and as sharp as a two-edged sword.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Her feet go down to death. Her steps lead straight to Sheol.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She gives no thought to the way of life. Her ways are crooked, and she doesn’t know it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now therefore, my sons, listen to me. Don’t depart from the words of my mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Remove your way far from her. Don’t come near the door of her house,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: lest you give your honor to others, and your years to the cruel one;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: lest strangers feast on your wealth, and your labors enrich another man’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You will groan at your latter end, when your flesh and your body are consumed,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and say, “How I have hated instruction, and my heart despised reproof;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: neither have I obeyed the voice of my teachers, nor turned my ear to those who instructed me!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have come to the brink of utter ruin, among the gathered assembly.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Drink water out of your own cistern, running water out of your own well.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Should your springs overflow in the streets, streams of water in the public squares?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let them be for yourself alone, not for strangers with you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let your spring be blessed. Rejoice in the wife of your youth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A loving doe and a graceful deer — let her breasts satisfy you at all times. Be captivated always with her love.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For why should you, my son, be captivated with an adulteress? Why embrace the bosom of another?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the ways of man are before Yahweh’s eyes. He examines all his paths.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The evil deeds of the wicked ensnare him. The cords of his sin hold him firmly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will die for lack of instruction. In the greatness of his folly, he will go astray.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My son, if you have become collateral for your neighbor, if you have struck your hands in pledge for a stranger;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You are trapped by the words of your mouth. You are ensnared with the words of your mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Do this now, my son, and deliver yourself, since you have come into the hand of your neighbor. Go, humble yourself. Press your plea with your neighbor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Give no sleep to your eyes, nor slumber to your eyelids.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Free yourself, like a gazelle from the hand of the hunter, like a bird from the snare of the fowler.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Go to the ant, you sluggard. Consider her ways, and be wise;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: which having no chief, overseer, or ruler,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: provides her bread in the summer, and gathers her food in the harvest.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: How long will you sleep, sluggard? When will you arise out of your sleep?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to sleep:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: so your poverty will come as a robber, and your scarcity as an armed man.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A worthless person, a man of iniquity, is he who walks with a perverse mouth;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who winks with his eyes, who signals with his feet, who motions with his fingers;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: in whose heart is perverseness, who devises evil continually, who always sows discord.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore his calamity will come suddenly. He will be broken suddenly, and that without remedy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There are six things which Yahweh hates; yes, seven which are an abomination to him:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: haughty eyes, a lying tongue, hands that shed innocent blood;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: a heart that devises wicked schemes, feet that are swift in running to mischief,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: a false witness who utters lies, and he who sows discord among brothers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My son, keep your father’s commandment, and don’t forsake your mother’s teaching.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Bind them continually on your heart. Tie them around your neck.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When you walk, it will lead you. When you sleep, it will watch over you. When you awake, it will talk with you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the commandment is a lamp, and the law is light. Reproofs of instruction are the way of life,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to keep you from the immoral woman, from the flattery of the wayward wife’s tongue.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t lust after her beauty in your heart, neither let her captivate you with her eyelids.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For a prostitute reduces you to a piece of bread. The adulteress hunts for your precious life.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Can a man scoop fire into his lap, and his clothes not be burned?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Or can one walk on hot coals, and his feet not be scorched?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So is he who goes in to his neighbor’s wife. Whoever touches her will not be unpunished.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Men don’t despise a thief, if he steals to satisfy himself when he is hungry:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but if he is found, he shall restore seven times. He shall give all the wealth of his house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who commits adultery with a woman is void of understanding. He who does it destroys his own soul.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will get wounds and dishonor. His reproach will not be wiped away.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For jealousy arouses the fury of the husband. He won’t spare in the day of vengeance.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He won’t regard any ransom, neither will he rest content, though you give many gifts.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My son, keep my words. Lay up my commandments within you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Keep my commandments and live! Guard my teaching as the apple of your eye.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Bind them on your fingers. Write them on the tablet of your heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Tell wisdom, “You are my sister.” Call understanding your relative,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that they may keep you from the strange woman, from the foreigner who flatters with her words.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For at the window of my house, I looked out through my lattice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I saw among the simple ones. I discerned among the youths a young man void of understanding,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: passing through the street near her corner, he went the way to her house,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: in the twilight, in the evening of the day, in the middle of the night and in the darkness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, there a woman met him with the attire of a prostitute, and with crafty intent.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She is loud and defiant. Her feet don’t stay in her house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now she is in the streets, now in the squares, and lurking at every corner.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So she caught him, and kissed him. With an impudent face she said to him:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Sacrifices of peace offerings are with me. Today I have paid my vows.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore I came out to meet you, to diligently seek your face, and I have found you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have spread my couch with carpets of tapestry, with striped cloths of the yarn of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Come, let’s take our fill of loving until the morning. Let’s solace ourselves with loving.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For my husband isn’t at home. He has gone on a long journey.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He has taken a bag of money with him. He will come home at the full moon.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: With persuasive words, she led him astray. With the flattering of her lips, she seduced him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He followed her immediately, as an ox goes to the slaughter, as a fool stepping into a noose.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Until an arrow strikes through his liver, as a bird hurries to the snare, and doesn’t know that it will cost his life.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now therefore, sons, listen to me. Pay attention to the words of my mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t let your heart turn to her ways. Don’t go astray in her paths,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for she has thrown down many wounded. Yes, all her slain are a mighty army.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Her house is the way to Sheol,  going down to the rooms of death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Doesn’t wisdom cry out? Doesn’t understanding raise her voice?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On the top of high places by the way, where the paths meet, she stands.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Beside the gates, at the entry of the city, at the entry doors, she cries aloud:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “To you men, I call! I send my voice to the sons of mankind.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You simple, understand prudence. You fools, be of an understanding heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hear, for I will speak excellent things. The opening of my lips is for right things.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For my mouth speaks truth. Wickedness is an abomination to my lips.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the words of my mouth are in righteousness. There is nothing crooked or perverse in them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They are all plain to him who understands, right to those who find knowledge.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Receive my instruction rather than silver; knowledge rather than choice gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For wisdom is better than rubies. All the things that may be desired can’t be compared to it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “I, wisdom, have made prudence my dwelling. Find out knowledge and discretion.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The fear of Yahweh is to hate evil. I hate pride, arrogance, the evil way, and the perverse mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Counsel and sound knowledge are mine. I have understanding and power.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: By me kings reign, and princes decree justice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: By me princes rule; nobles, and all the righteous rulers of the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I love those who love me. Those who seek me diligently will find me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: With me are riches, honor, enduring wealth, and prosperity.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My fruit is better than gold, yes, than fine gold; my yield than choice silver.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I walk in the way of righteousness, in the middle of the paths of justice;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: That I may give wealth to those who love me. I fill their treasuries.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Yahweh possessed me in the beginning of his work, before his deeds of old.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I was set up from everlasting, from the beginning, before the earth existed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When there were no depths, I was born, when there were no springs abounding with water.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Before the mountains were settled in place, before the hills, I was born;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: while as yet he had not made the earth, nor the fields, nor the beginning of the dust of the world.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he established the heavens, I was there; when he set a circle on the surface of the deep,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: when he established the clouds above, when the springs of the deep became strong,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: when he gave to the sea its boundary, that the waters should not violate his commandment, when he marked out the foundations of the earth;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then I was the craftsman by his side. I was a delight day by day, always rejoicing before him,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Rejoicing in his whole world. My delight was with the sons of men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now therefore, my sons, listen to me, for blessed are those who keep my ways.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hear instruction, and be wise. Don’t refuse it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Blessed is the man who hears me, watching daily at my gates, waiting at my door posts.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For whoever finds me, finds life, and will obtain favor from Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he who sins against me wrongs his own soul. All those who hate me love death.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Wisdom has built her house. She has carved out her seven pillars.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She has prepared her meat. She has mixed her wine. She has also set her table.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She has sent out her maidens. She cries from the highest places of the city:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Whoever is simple, let him turn in here!” As for him who is void of understanding, she says to him,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Come, eat some of my bread, Drink some of the wine which I have mixed!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Leave your simple ways, and live. Walk in the way of understanding.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who corrects a mocker invites insult. He who reproves a wicked man invites abuse.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t reprove a scoffer, lest he hate you. Reprove a wise man, and he will love you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Instruct a wise man, and he will be still wiser. Teach a righteous man, and he will increase in learning.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The fear of Yahweh is the beginning of wisdom. The knowledge of the Holy One is understanding.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For by me your days will be multiplied. The years of your life will be increased.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you are wise, you are wise for yourself. If you mock, you alone will bear it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The foolish woman is loud, undisciplined, and knows nothing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She sits at the door of her house, on a seat in the high places of the city,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: To call to those who pass by, who go straight on their ways,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Whoever is simple, let him turn in here.” as for him who is void of understanding, she says to him,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Stolen water is sweet. Food eaten in secret is pleasant.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he doesn’t know that the departed spirits are there, that her guests are in the depths of Sheol.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The proverbs of Solomon. A wise son makes a glad father; but a foolish son brings grief to his mother.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Treasures of wickedness profit nothing, but righteousness delivers from death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh will not allow the soul of the righteous to go hungry, but he thrusts away the desire of the wicked.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He becomes poor who works with a lazy hand, but the hand of the diligent brings wealth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who gathers in summer is a wise son, but he who sleeps during the harvest is a son who causes shame.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Blessings are on the head of the righteous, but violence covers the mouth of the wicked.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The memory of the righteous is blessed, but the name of the wicked will rot.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The wise in heart accept commandments, but a chattering fool will fall.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who walks blamelessly walks surely, but he who perverts his ways will be found out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One winking with the eye causes sorrow, but a chattering fool will fall.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The mouth of the righteous is a spring of life, but violence covers the mouth of the wicked.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hatred stirs up strife, but love covers all wrongs.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Wisdom is found on the lips of him who has discernment, but a rod is for the back of him who is void of understanding.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Wise men lay up knowledge, but the mouth of the foolish is near ruin.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The rich man’s wealth is his strong city. The destruction of the poor is their poverty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The labor of the righteous leads to life. The increase of the wicked leads to sin.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He is in the way of life who heeds correction, but he who forsakes reproof leads others astray.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who hides hatred has lying lips. He who utters a slander is a fool.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the multitude of words there is no lack of disobedience, but he who restrains his lips does wisely.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The tongue of the righteous is like choice silver. The heart of the wicked is of little worth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The lips of the righteous feed many, but the foolish die for lack of understanding.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s blessing brings wealth, and he adds no trouble to it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It is a fool’s pleasure to do wickedness, but wisdom is a man of understanding’s pleasure.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: What the wicked fear, will overtake them, but the desire of the righteous will be granted.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the whirlwind passes, the wicked is no more; but the righteous stand firm forever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As vinegar to the teeth, and as smoke to the eyes, so is the sluggard to those who send him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The fear of Yahweh prolongs days, but the years of the wicked shall be shortened.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The prospect of the righteous is joy, but the hope of the wicked will perish.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The way of Yahweh is a stronghold to the upright, but it is a destruction to the workers of iniquity.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The righteous will never be removed, but the wicked will not dwell in the land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The mouth of the righteous produces wisdom, but the perverse tongue will be cut off.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable, but the mouth of the wicked is perverse.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A false balance is an abomination to Yahweh, but accurate weights are his delight.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When pride comes, then comes shame, but with humility comes wisdom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The integrity of the upright shall guide them, but the perverseness of the treacherous shall destroy them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Riches don’t profit in the day of wrath, but righteousness delivers from death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The righteousness of the blameless will direct his way, but the wicked shall fall by his own wickedness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The righteousness of the upright shall deliver them, but the unfaithful will be trapped by evil desires.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When a wicked man dies, hope perishes, and expectation of power comes to nothing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A righteous person is delivered out of trouble, and the wicked takes his place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: With his mouth the godless man destroys his neighbor, but the righteous will be delivered through knowledge.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When it goes well with the righteous, the city rejoices. When the wicked perish, there is shouting.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: By the blessing of the upright, the city is exalted, but it is overthrown by the mouth of the wicked.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One who despises his neighbor is void of wisdom, but a man of understanding holds his peace.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One who brings gossip betrays a confidence, but one who is of a trustworthy spirit is one who keeps a secret.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Where there is no wise guidance, the nation falls, but in the multitude of counselors there is victory.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who is collateral for a stranger will suffer for it, but he who refuses pledges of collateral is secure.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A gracious woman obtains honor, but violent men obtain riches.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The merciful man does good to his own soul, but he who is cruel troubles his own flesh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Wicked people earn deceitful wages, but one who sows righteousness reaps a sure reward.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who is truly righteous gets life. He who pursues evil gets death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who are perverse in heart are an abomination to Yahweh, but those whose ways are blameless are his delight.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Most certainly, the evil man will not be unpunished, but the offspring  of the righteous will be delivered.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Like a gold ring in a pig’s snout, is a beautiful woman who lacks discretion.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The desire of the righteous is only good. The expectation of the wicked is wrath.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is one who scatters, and increases yet more. There is one who withholds more than is appropriate, but gains poverty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The liberal soul shall be made fat. He who waters shall be watered also himself.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: People curse someone who withholds grain, but blessing will be on the head of him who sells it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who diligently seeks good seeks favor, but he who searches after evil, it shall come to him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who trusts in his riches will fall, but the righteous shall flourish as the green leaf.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who troubles his own house shall inherit the wind. The foolish shall be servant to the wise of heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life. He who is wise wins souls.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, the righteous shall be repaid in the earth; how much more the wicked and the sinner!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever loves correction loves knowledge, but he who hates reproof is stupid.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A good man shall obtain favor from Yahweh, but he will condemn a man of wicked devices.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A man shall not be established by wickedness, but the root of the righteous shall not be moved.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A worthy woman is the crown of her husband, but a disgraceful wife is as rottenness in his bones.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The thoughts of the righteous are just, but the advice of the wicked is deceitful.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The words of the wicked are about lying in wait for blood, but the speech of the upright rescues them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The wicked are overthrown, and are no more, but the house of the righteous shall stand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A man shall be commended according to his wisdom, but he who has a warped mind shall be despised.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Better is he who is lightly esteemed, and has a servant, than he who honors himself, and lacks bread.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A righteous man respects the life of his animal, but the tender mercies of the wicked are cruel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who tills his land shall have plenty of bread, but he who chases fantasies is void of understanding.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The wicked desires the plunder of evil men, but the root of the righteous flourishes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: An evil man is trapped by sinfulness of lips, but the righteous shall come out of trouble.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A man shall be satisfied with good by the fruit of his mouth. The work of a man’s hands shall be rewarded to him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The way of a fool is right in his own eyes, but he who is wise listens to counsel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A fool shows his annoyance the same day, but one who overlooks an insult is prudent.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who is truthful testifies honestly, but a false witness lies.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is one who speaks rashly like the piercing of a sword, but the tongue of the wise heals.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Truth’s lips will be established forever, but a lying tongue is only momentary.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Deceit is in the heart of those who plot evil, but joy comes to the promoters of peace.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: No mischief shall happen to the righteous, but the wicked shall be filled with evil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Lying lips are an abomination to Yahweh, but those who do the truth are his delight.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A prudent man keeps his knowledge, but the hearts of fools proclaim foolishness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The hands of the diligent ones shall rule, but laziness ends in slave labor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Anxiety in a man’s heart weighs it down, but a kind word makes it glad.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A righteous person is cautious in friendship, but the way of the wicked leads them astray.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The slothful man doesn’t roast his game, but the possessions of diligent men are prized.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the way of righteousness is life; in its path there is no death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A wise son listens to his father’s instruction, but a scoffer doesn’t listen to rebuke.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: By the fruit of his lips, a man enjoys good things; but the unfaithful crave violence.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who guards his mouth guards his soul. One who opens wide his lips comes to ruin.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The soul of the sluggard desires, and has nothing, but the desire of the diligent shall be fully satisfied.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A righteous man hates lies, but a wicked man brings shame and disgrace.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Righteousness guards the way of integrity, but wickedness overthrows the sinner.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There are some who pretend to be rich, yet have nothing. There are some who pretend to be poor, yet have great wealth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The ransom of a man’s life is his riches, but the poor hear no threats.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The light of the righteous shines brightly, but the lamp of the wicked is snuffed out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pride only breeds quarrels, but with ones who take advice is wisdom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Wealth gained dishonestly dwindles away, but he who gathers by hand makes it grow.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hope deferred makes the heart sick, but when longing is fulfilled, it is a tree of life.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever despises instruction will pay for it, but he who respects a command will be rewarded.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The teaching of the wise is a spring of life, to turn from the snares of death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Good understanding wins favor; but the way of the unfaithful is hard.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Every prudent man acts from knowledge, but a fool exposes folly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A wicked messenger falls into trouble, but a trustworthy envoy gains healing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Poverty and shame come to him who refuses discipline, but he who heeds correction shall be honored.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Longing fulfilled is sweet to the soul, but fools detest turning from evil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One who walks with wise men grows wise, but a companion of fools suffers harm.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Misfortune pursues sinners, but prosperity rewards the righteous.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A good man leaves an inheritance to his children’s children, but the wealth of the sinner is stored for the righteous.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: An abundance of food is in poor people’s fields, but injustice sweeps it away.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One who spares the rod hates his son, but one who loves him is careful to discipline him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The righteous one eats to the satisfying of his soul, but the belly of the wicked goes hungry.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Every wise woman builds her house, but the foolish one tears it down with her own hands.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who walks in his uprightness fears Yahweh, but he who is perverse in his ways despises him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The fool’s talk brings a rod to his back, but the lips of the wise protect them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Where no oxen are, the crib is clean, but much increase is by the strength of the ox.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A truthful witness will not lie, but a false witness pours out lies.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A scoffer seeks wisdom, and doesn’t find it, but knowledge comes easily to a discerning person.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Stay away from a foolish man, for you won’t find knowledge on his lips.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The wisdom of the prudent is to think about his way, but the folly of fools is deceit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Fools mock at making atonement for sins, but among the upright there is good will.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The heart knows its own bitterness and joy; he will not share these with a stranger.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The house of the wicked will be overthrown, but the tent of the upright will flourish.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is a way which seems right to a man, but in the end it leads to death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Even in laughter the heart may be sorrowful, and mirth may end in heaviness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The unfaithful will be repaid for his own ways; likewise a good man will be rewarded for his ways.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A simple man believes everything, but the prudent man carefully considers his ways.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A wise man fears and shuns evil, but the fool is hot headed and reckless.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who is quick to become angry will commit folly, and a crafty man is hated.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The simple inherit folly, but the prudent are crowned with knowledge.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The evil bow down before the good, and the wicked at the gates of the righteous.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The poor person is shunned even by his own neighbor, but the rich person has many friends.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who despises his neighbor sins, but blessed is he who has pity on the poor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t they go astray who plot evil? But love and faithfulness belong to those who plan good.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In all hard work there is profit, but the talk of the lips leads only to poverty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The crown of the wise is their riches, but the folly of fools crowns them with folly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A truthful witness saves souls, but a false witness is deceitful.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the fear of Yahweh is a secure fortress, and he will be a refuge for his children.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The fear of Yahweh is a fountain of life, turning people from the snares of death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the multitude of people is the king’s glory, but in the lack of people is the destruction of the prince.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who is slow to anger has great understanding, but he who has a quick temper displays folly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The life of the body is a heart at peace, but envy rots the bones.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who oppresses the poor shows contempt for his Maker, but he who is kind to the needy honors him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The wicked is brought down in his calamity, but in death, the righteous has a refuge.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Wisdom rests in the heart of one who has understanding, and is even made known in the inward part of fools.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Righteousness exalts a nation, but sin is a disgrace to any people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king’s favor is toward a servant who deals wisely, but his wrath is toward one who causes shame.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A gentle answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The tongue of the wise commends knowledge, but the mouth of fools gush out folly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s eyes are everywhere, keeping watch on the evil and the good.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A gentle tongue is a tree of life, but deceit in it crushes the spirit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A fool despises his father’s correction, but he who heeds reproof shows prudence.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the house of the righteous is much treasure, but the income of the wicked brings trouble.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The lips of the wise spread knowledge; not so with the heart of fools.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sacrifice made by the wicked is an abomination to Yahweh, but the prayer of the upright is his delight.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The way of the wicked is an abomination to Yahweh, but he loves him who follows after righteousness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is stern discipline for one who forsakes the way: whoever hates reproof shall die.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Sheol  and Abaddon are before Yahweh — how much more then the hearts of the children of men!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A scoffer doesn’t love to be reproved; he will not go to the wise.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A glad heart makes a cheerful face; but an aching heart breaks the spirit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The heart of one who has understanding seeks knowledge, but the mouths of fools feed on folly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the days of the afflicted are wretched, but one who has a cheerful heart enjoys a continual feast.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Better is little, with the fear of Yahweh, than great treasure with trouble.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Better is a dinner of herbs, where love is, than a fattened calf with hatred.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A wrathful man stirs up contention, but one who is slow to anger appeases strife.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The way of the sluggard is like a thorn patch, but the path of the upright is a highway.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A wise son makes a father glad, but a foolish man despises his mother.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Folly is joy to one who is void of wisdom, but a man of understanding keeps his way straight.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Where there is no counsel, plans fail; but in a multitude of counselors they are established.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Joy comes to a man with the reply of his mouth. How good is a word at the right time!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The path of life leads upward for the wise, to keep him from going downward to Sheol.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh will uproot the house of the proud, but he will keep the widow’s borders intact.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh detests the thoughts of the wicked, but the thoughts of the pure are pleasing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who is greedy for gain troubles his own house, but he who hates bribes will live.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The heart of the righteous weighs answers, but the mouth of the wicked gushes out evil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh is far from the wicked, but he hears the prayer of the righteous.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The light of the eyes rejoices the heart. Good news gives health to the bones.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The ear that listens to reproof lives, and will be at home among the wise.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who refuses correction despises his own soul, but he who listens to reproof gets understanding.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The fear of Yahweh teaches wisdom. Before honor is humility.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The plans of the heart belong to man, but the answer of the tongue is from Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the ways of a man are clean in his own eyes; but Yahweh weighs the motives.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Commit your deeds to Yahweh, and your plans shall succeed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh has made everything for its own end — yes, even the wicked for the day of evil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Everyone who is proud in heart is an abomination to Yahweh: they shall certainly not be unpunished.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: By mercy and truth iniquity is atoned for. By the fear of Yahweh men depart from evil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When a man’s ways please Yahweh, he makes even his enemies to be at peace with him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Better is a little with righteousness, than great revenues with injustice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A man’s heart plans his course, but Yahweh directs his steps.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Inspired judgments are on the lips of the king. He shall not betray his mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Honest balances and scales are Yahweh’s; all the weights in the bag are his work.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It is an abomination for kings to do wrong, for the throne is established by righteousness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Righteous lips are the delight of kings. They value one who speaks the truth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king’s wrath is a messenger of death, but a wise man will pacify it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the light of the king’s face is life. His favor is like a cloud of the spring rain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: How much better it is to get wisdom than gold! Yes, to get understanding is to be chosen rather than silver.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The highway of the upright is to depart from evil. He who keeps his way preserves his soul.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pride goes before destruction, and a haughty spirit before a fall.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It is better to be of a lowly spirit with the poor, than to divide the plunder with the proud.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who heeds the Word finds prosperity. Whoever trusts in Yahweh is blessed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The wise in heart shall be called prudent. Pleasantness of the lips promotes instruction.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Understanding is a fountain of life to one who has it, but the punishment of fools is their folly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The heart of the wise instructs his mouth, and adds learning to his lips.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pleasant words are a honeycomb, sweet to the soul, and health to the bones.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is a way which seems right to a man, but in the end it leads to death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The appetite of the laboring man labors for him; for his mouth urges him on.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A worthless man devises mischief. His speech is like a scorching fire.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A perverse man stirs up strife. A whisperer separates close friends.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A man of violence entices his neighbor, and leads him in a way that is not good.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One who winks his eyes to plot perversities, one who compresses his lips, is bent on evil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Gray hair is a crown of glory. It is attained by a life of righteousness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One who is slow to anger is better than the mighty; one who rules his spirit, than he who takes a city.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The lot is cast into the lap, but its every decision is from Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Better is a dry morsel with quietness, than a house full of feasting with strife.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A servant who deals wisely will rule over a son who causes shame, and shall have a part in the inheritance among the brothers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The refining pot is for silver, and the furnace for gold, but Yahweh tests the hearts.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: An evildoer heeds wicked lips. A liar gives ear to a mischievous tongue.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever mocks the poor reproaches his Maker. He who is glad at calamity shall not be unpunished.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Children’s children are the crown of old men; the glory of children are their parents.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Arrogant speech isn’t fitting for a fool, much less do lying lips fit a prince.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A bribe is a precious stone in the eyes of him who gives it; wherever he turns, he prospers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who covers an offense promotes love; but he who repeats a matter separates best friends.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A rebuke enters deeper into one who has understanding than a hundred lashes into a fool.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: An evil man seeks only rebellion; therefore a cruel messenger shall be sent against him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let a bear robbed of her cubs meet a man, rather than a fool in his folly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever rewards evil for good, evil shall not depart from his house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The beginning of strife is like breaching a dam, therefore stop contention before quarreling breaks out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who justifies the wicked, and he who condemns the righteous, both of them alike are an abomination to Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why is there money in the hand of a fool to buy wisdom, since he has no understanding?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A friend loves at all times; and a brother is born for adversity.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A man void of understanding strikes hands, and becomes collateral in the presence of his neighbor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who loves disobedience loves strife. One who builds a high gate seeks destruction.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One who has a perverse heart doesn’t find prosperity, and one who has a deceitful tongue falls into trouble.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who becomes the father of a fool grieves. The father of a fool has no joy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A cheerful heart makes good medicine, but a crushed spirit dries up the bones.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A wicked man receives a bribe in secret, to pervert the ways of justice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Wisdom is before the face of one who has understanding, but the eyes of a fool wander to the ends of the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A foolish son brings grief to his father, and bitterness to her who bore him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Also to punish the righteous is not good, nor to flog officials for their integrity.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who spares his words has knowledge. He who is even tempered is a man of understanding.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Even a fool, when he keeps silent, is counted wise. When he shuts his lips, he is thought to be discerning.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: An unfriendly man pursues selfishness, and defies all sound judgment.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A fool has no delight in understanding, but only in revealing his own opinion.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When wickedness comes, contempt also comes, and with shame comes disgrace.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The words of a man’s mouth are like deep waters. The fountain of wisdom is like a flowing brook.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: To be partial to the faces of the wicked is not good, nor to deprive the innocent of justice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A fool’s lips come into strife, and his mouth invites beatings.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A fool’s mouth is his destruction, and his lips are a snare to his soul.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The words of a gossip are like dainty morsels: they go down into a person’s innermost parts.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One who is slack in his work is brother to him who is a master of destruction.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s name is a strong tower: the righteous run to him, and are safe.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The rich man’s wealth is his strong city, like an unscalable wall in his own imagination.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Before destruction the heart of man is proud, but before honor is humility.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who gives answer before he hears, that is folly and shame to him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A man’s spirit will sustain him in sickness, but a crushed spirit, who can bear?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The heart of the discerning gets knowledge. The ear of the wise seeks knowledge.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A man’s gift makes room for him, and brings him before great men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who pleads his cause first seems right; until another comes and questions him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The lot settles disputes, and keeps strong ones apart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A brother offended is more difficult than a fortified city; and disputes are like the bars of a fortress.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A man’s stomach is filled with the fruit of his mouth. With the harvest of his lips he is satisfied.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Death and life are in the power of the tongue; those who love it will eat its fruit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever finds a wife finds a good thing, and obtains favor of Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The poor plead for mercy, but the rich answer harshly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A man of many companions may be ruined, but there is a friend who sticks closer than a brother.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Better is the poor who walks in his integrity than he who is perverse in his lips and is a fool.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It isn’t good to have zeal without knowledge; nor being hasty with one’s feet and missing the way.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The foolishness of man subverts his way; his heart rages against Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Wealth adds many friends, but the poor is separated from his friend.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A false witness shall not be unpunished. He who pours out lies shall not go free.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Many will entreat the favor of a ruler, and everyone is a friend to a man who gives gifts.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the relatives of the poor shun him: how much more do his friends avoid him! He pursues them with pleas, but they are gone.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who gets wisdom loves his own soul. He who keeps understanding shall find good.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A false witness shall not be unpunished. He who utters lies shall perish.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Delicate living is not appropriate for a fool, much less for a servant to have rule over princes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The discretion of a man makes him slow to anger. It is his glory to overlook an offense.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king’s wrath is like the roaring of a lion, but his favor is like dew on the grass.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A foolish son is the calamity of his father. A wife’s quarrels are a continual dripping.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: House and riches are an inheritance from fathers, but a prudent wife is from Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Slothfulness casts into a deep sleep. The idle soul shall suffer hunger.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who keeps the commandment keeps his soul, but he who is contemptuous in his ways shall die.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who has pity on the poor lends to Yahweh; he will reward him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Discipline your son, for there is hope; don’t be a willing party to his death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A hot-tempered man must pay the penalty, for if you rescue him, you must do it again.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Listen to counsel and receive instruction, that you may be wise in your latter end.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There are many plans in a man’s heart, but Yahweh’s counsel will prevail.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: That which makes a man to be desired is his kindness. A poor man is better than a liar.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The fear of Yahweh leads to life, then contentment; he rests and will not be touched by trouble.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sluggard buries his hand in the dish; he will not so much as bring it to his mouth again.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Flog a scoffer, and the simple will learn prudence; rebuke one who has understanding, and he will gain knowledge.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who robs his father and drives away his mother, is a son who causes shame and brings reproach.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you stop listening to instruction, my son, you will stray from the words of knowledge.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A corrupt witness mocks justice, and the mouth of the wicked gulps down iniquity.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Penalties are prepared for scoffers, and beatings for the backs of fools.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Wine is a mocker, and beer is a brawler. Whoever is led astray by them is not wise.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The terror of a king is like the roaring of a lion. He who provokes him to anger forfeits his own life.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It is an honor for a man to keep aloof from strife; but every fool will be quarreling.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sluggard will not plow by reason of the winter; therefore he shall beg in harvest, and have nothing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Counsel in the heart of man is like deep water; but a man of understanding will draw it out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Many men claim to be men of unfailing love, but who can find a faithful man?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A righteous man walks in integrity. Blessed are his children after him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A king who sits on the throne of judgment scatters away all evil with his eyes.

translations.name: World English Bible
