translations.text: Who can say, “I have made my heart pure. I am clean and without sin?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Differing weights and differing measures, both of them alike are an abomination to Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Even a child makes himself known by his doings, whether his work is pure, and whether it is right.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The hearing ear, and the seeing eye, Yahweh has made even both of them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t love sleep, lest you come to poverty. Open your eyes, and you shall be satisfied with bread.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “It’s no good, it’s no good,” says the buyer; but when he is gone his way, then he boasts.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is gold and abundance of rubies; but the lips of knowledge are a rare jewel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Take the garment of one who puts up collateral for a stranger; and hold him in pledge for a wayward woman.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Fraudulent food is sweet to a man, but afterwards his mouth is filled with gravel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Plans are established by advice; by wise guidance you wage war!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who goes about as a tale-bearer reveals secrets; therefore don’t keep company with him who opens wide his lips.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever curses his father or his mother, his lamp shall be put out in blackness of darkness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: An inheritance quickly gained at the beginning, won’t be blessed in the end.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t say, “I will pay back evil.” Wait for Yahweh, and he will save you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh detests differing weights, and dishonest scales are not pleasing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A man’s steps are from Yahweh; how then can man understand his way?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It is a snare to a man to make a rash dedication, then later to consider his vows.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A wise king winnows out the wicked, and drives the threshing wheel over them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The spirit of man is Yahweh’s lamp, searching all his innermost parts.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Love and faithfulness keep the king safe. His throne is sustained by love.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The glory of young men is their strength. The splendor of old men is their gray hair.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Wounding blows cleanse away evil, and beatings purge the innermost parts.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king’s heart is in Yahweh’s hand like the watercourses. He turns it wherever he desires.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Every way of a man is right in his own eyes, but Yahweh weighs the hearts.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: To do righteousness and justice is more acceptable to Yahweh than sacrifice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A high look, and a proud heart, the lamp of the wicked, is sin.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The plans of the diligent surely lead to profit; and everyone who is hasty surely rushes to poverty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Getting treasures by a lying tongue is a fleeting vapor for those who seek death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The violence of the wicked will drive them away, because they refuse to do what is right.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The way of the guilty is devious, but the conduct of the innocent is upright.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It is better to dwell in the corner of the housetop, than to share a house with a contentious woman.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The soul of the wicked desires evil; his neighbor finds no mercy in his eyes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the mocker is punished, the simple gains wisdom. When the wise is instructed, he receives knowledge.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Righteous One considers the house of the wicked, and brings the wicked to ruin.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever stops his ears at the cry of the poor, he will also cry out, but shall not be heard.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A gift in secret pacifies anger; and a bribe in the cloak, strong wrath.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It is joy to the righteous to do justice; but it is a destruction to the workers of iniquity.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The man who wanders out of the way of understanding shall rest in the assembly of the departed spirits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who loves pleasure shall be a poor man. He who loves wine and oil shall not be rich.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The wicked is a ransom for the righteous; the treacherous for the upright.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It is better to dwell in a desert land, than with a contentious and fretful woman.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is precious treasure and oil in the dwelling of the wise; but a foolish man swallows it up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who follows after righteousness and kindness finds life, righteousness, and honor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A wise man scales the city of the mighty, and brings down the strength of its confidence.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever guards his mouth and his tongue keeps his soul from troubles.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The proud and haughty man, “scoffer” is his name; he works in the arrogance of pride.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The desire of the sluggard kills him, for his hands refuse to labor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There are those who covet greedily all day long; but the righteous give and don’t withhold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination; how much more, when he brings it with a wicked mind!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A false witness will perish, and a man who listens speaks to eternity.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A wicked man hardens his face; but as for the upright, he establishes his ways.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is no wisdom nor understanding nor counsel against Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The horse is prepared for the day of battle; but victory is with Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A good name is more desirable than great riches, and loving favor is better than silver and gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The rich and the poor have this in common: Yahweh is the maker of them all.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A prudent man sees danger, and hides himself; but the simple pass on, and suffer for it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The result of humility and the fear of Yahweh is wealth, honor, and life.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thorns and snares are in the path of the wicked: whoever guards his soul stays from them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Train up a child in the way he should go, and when he is old he will not depart from it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The rich rule over the poor. The borrower is servant to the lender.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who sows wickedness reaps trouble, and the rod of his fury will be destroyed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who has a generous eye will be blessed; for he shares his food with the poor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Drive out the mocker, and strife will go out; yes, quarrels and insults will stop.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who loves purity of heart and speaks gracefully is the king’s friend.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s eyes watch over knowledge; but he frustrates the words of the unfaithful.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sluggard says, “There is a lion outside! I will be killed in the streets!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The mouth of an adulteress is a deep pit. He who is under Yahweh’s wrath will fall into it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Folly is bound up in the heart of a child: the rod of discipline drives it far from him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever oppresses the poor for his own increase and whoever gives to the rich, both come to poverty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Turn your ear, and listen to the words of the wise. Apply your heart to my teaching.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For it is a pleasant thing if you keep them within you, if all of them are ready on your lips.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: That your trust may be in Yahweh, I teach you today, even you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Haven’t I written to you thirty excellent things of counsel and knowledge,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: To teach you truth, reliable words, to give sound answers to the ones who sent you?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t exploit the poor, because he is poor; and don’t crush the needy in court;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for Yahweh will plead their case, and plunder the life of those who plunder them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t befriend a hot-tempered man, and don’t associate with one who harbors anger:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: lest you learn his ways, and ensnare your soul.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t you be one of those who strike hands, of those who are collateral for debts.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you don’t have means to pay, why should he take away your bed from under you?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t move the ancient boundary stone, which your fathers have set up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Do you see a man skilled in his work? He will serve kings. He won’t serve obscure men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When you sit to eat with a ruler, consider diligently what is before you;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: put a knife to your throat, if you are a man given to appetite.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t be desirous of his dainties, since they are deceitful food.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t weary yourself to be rich. In your wisdom, show restraint.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why do you set your eyes on that which is not? For it certainly sprouts wings like an eagle and flies in the sky.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t eat the food of him who has a stingy eye, and don’t crave his delicacies:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for as he thinks about the cost, so he is. “Eat and drink!” he says to you, but his heart is not with you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The morsel which you have eaten you shall vomit up, and lose your good words.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t speak in the ears of a fool, for he will despise the wisdom of your words.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t move the ancient boundary stone. Don’t encroach on the fields of the fatherless:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for their Defender is strong. He will plead their case against you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Apply your heart to instruction, and your ears to the words of knowledge.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t withhold correction from a child. If you punish him with the rod, he will not die.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Punish him with the rod, and save his soul from Sheol.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My son, if your heart is wise, then my heart will be glad, even mine:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: yes, my heart will rejoice, when your lips speak what is right.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t let your heart envy sinners; but rather fear Yahweh all day long.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Indeed surely there is a future hope, and your hope will not be cut off.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Listen, my son, and be wise, and keep your heart on the right path!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t be among ones drinking too much wine, or those who gorge themselves on meat:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for the drunkard and the glutton shall become poor; and drowsiness clothes them in rags.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Listen to your father who gave you life, and don’t despise your mother when she is old.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Buy the truth, and don’t sell it. Get wisdom, discipline, and understanding.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The father of the righteous has great joy. Whoever fathers a wise child delights in him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let your father and your mother be glad! Let her who bore you rejoice!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My son, give me your heart; and let your eyes keep in my ways.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For a prostitute is a deep pit; and a wayward wife is a narrow well.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, she lies in wait like a robber, and increases the unfaithful among men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who has woe? Who has sorrow? Who has strife? Who has complaints? Who has needless bruises? Who has bloodshot eyes?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who stay long at the wine; those who go to seek out mixed wine.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t look at the wine when it is red, when it sparkles in the cup, when it goes down smoothly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the end, it bites like a snake, and poisons like a viper.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your eyes will see strange things, and your mind will imagine confusing things.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, you will be as he who lies down in the middle of the sea, or as he who lies on top of the rigging:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “They hit me, and I was not hurt! They beat me, and I don’t feel it! When will I wake up? I can do it again. I can find another.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t be envious of evil men; neither desire to be with them:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for their hearts plot violence, and their lips talk about mischief.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Through wisdom a house is built; by understanding it is established;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: by knowledge the rooms are filled with all rare and beautiful treasure.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A wise man has great power; and a knowledgeable man increases strength;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for by wise guidance you wage your war; and victory is in many advisors.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Wisdom is too high for a fool: he doesn’t open his mouth in the gate.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One who plots to do evil will be called a schemer.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The schemes of folly are sin. The mocker is detested by men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you falter in the time of trouble, your strength is small.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Rescue those who are being led away to death! Indeed, hold back those who are staggering to the slaughter!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you say, “Behold, we didn’t know this”; doesn’t he who weighs the hearts consider it? He who keeps your soul, doesn’t he know it? Shall he not render to every man according to his work?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My son, eat honey, for it is good; the droppings of the honeycomb, which are sweet to your taste:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: so you shall know wisdom to be to your soul; if you have found it, then there will be a reward, your hope will not be cut off.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t lay in wait, wicked man, against the habitation of the righteous. Don’t destroy his resting place:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for a righteous man falls seven times, and rises up again; but the wicked are overthrown by calamity.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t rejoice when your enemy falls. Don’t let your heart be glad when he is overthrown;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: lest Yahweh see it, and it displease him, and he turn away his wrath from him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t fret yourself because of evildoers; neither be envious of the wicked:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for there will be no reward to the evil man; and the lamp of the wicked shall be snuffed out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My son, fear Yahweh and the king. Don’t join those who are rebellious:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for their calamity will rise suddenly; the destruction from them both — who knows?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: These also are sayings of the wise. To show partiality in judgment is not good.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who says to the wicked, “You are righteous”; peoples will curse him, and nations will abhor him —

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but it will go well with those who convict the guilty, and a rich blessing will come on them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: An honest answer is like a kiss on the lips.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Prepare your work outside, and get your fields ready. Afterwards, build your house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t be a witness against your neighbor without cause. Don’t deceive with your lips.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t say, “I will do to him as he has done to me; I will render to the man according to his work.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I went by the field of the sluggard, by the vineyard of the man void of understanding;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, it was all grown over with thorns. Its surface was covered with nettles, and its stone wall was broken down.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then I saw, and considered well. I saw, and received instruction:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: a little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to sleep;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: so your poverty will come as a robber, and your want as an armed man.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: These also are proverbs of Solomon, which the men of Hezekiah king of Judah copied out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It is the glory of God to conceal a thing, but the glory of kings is to search out a matter.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As the heavens for height, and the earth for depth, so the hearts of kings are unsearchable.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Take away the dross from the silver, and material comes out for the refiner;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Take away the wicked from the king’s presence, and his throne will be established in righteousness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t exalt yourself in the presence of the king, or claim a place among great men;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for it is better that it be said to you, “Come up here,” than that you should be put lower in the presence of the prince, whom your eyes have seen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t be hasty in bringing charges to court. What will you do in the end when your neighbor shames you?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Debate your case with your neighbor, and don’t betray the confidence of another;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: lest one who hears it put you to shame, and your bad reputation never depart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A word fitly spoken is like apples of gold in settings of silver.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As an earring of gold, and an ornament of fine gold, so is a wise reprover to an obedient ear.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As the cold of snow in the time of harvest, so is a faithful messenger to those who send him; for he refreshes the soul of his masters.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As clouds and wind without rain, so is he who boasts of gifts deceptively.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: By patience a ruler is persuaded. A soft tongue breaks the bone.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Have you found honey? Eat as much as is sufficient for you, lest you eat too much, and vomit it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let your foot be seldom in your neighbor’s house, lest he be weary of you, and hate you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A man who gives false testimony against his neighbor is like a club, a sword, or a sharp arrow.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Confidence in someone unfaithful in time of trouble is like a bad tooth, or a lame foot.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As one who takes away a garment in cold weather, or vinegar on soda, so is one who sings songs to a heavy heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If your enemy is hungry, give him food to eat. If he is thirsty, give him water to drink:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for you will heap coals of fire on his head, and Yahweh will reward you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The north wind produces rain; so a backbiting tongue brings an angry face.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It is better to dwell in the corner of the housetop, than to share a house with a contentious woman.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Like cold water to a thirsty soul, so is good news from a far country.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Like a muddied spring, and a polluted well, so is a righteous man who gives way before the wicked.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It is not good to eat much honey; nor is it honorable to seek one’s own honor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Like a city that is broken down and without walls is a man whose spirit is without restraint.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Like snow in summer, and as rain in harvest, so honor is not fitting for a fool.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Like a fluttering sparrow, like a darting swallow, so the undeserved curse doesn’t come to rest.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A whip is for the horse, a bridle for the donkey, and a rod for the back of fools!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t answer a fool according to his folly, lest you also be like him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Answer a fool according to his folly, lest he be wise in his own eyes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One who sends a message by the hand of a fool is cutting off feet and drinking violence.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Like the legs of the lame that hang loose, so is a parable in the mouth of fools.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As one who binds a stone in a sling, so is he who gives honor to a fool.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Like a thorn bush that goes into the hand of a drunkard, so is a parable in the mouth of fools.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As an archer who wounds all, so is he who hires a fool or he who hires those who pass by.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As a dog that returns to his vomit, so is a fool who repeats his folly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Do you see a man wise in his own eyes? There is more hope for a fool than for him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sluggard says, “There is a lion in the road! A fierce lion roams the streets!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As the door turns on its hinges, so does the sluggard on his bed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sluggard buries his hand in the dish. He is too lazy to bring it back to his mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sluggard is wiser in his own eyes than seven men who answer with discretion.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Like one who grabs a dog’s ears is one who passes by and meddles in a quarrel not his own.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Like a madman who shoots torches, arrows, and death,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: is the man who deceives his neighbor and says, “Am I not joking?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For lack of wood a fire goes out. Without gossip, a quarrel dies down.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As coals are to hot embers, and wood to fire, so is a contentious man to kindling strife.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The words of a whisperer are as dainty morsels, they go down into the innermost parts.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Like silver dross on an earthen vessel are the lips of a fervent one with an evil heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A malicious man disguises himself with his lips, but he harbors evil in his heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When his speech is charming, don’t believe him; for there are seven abominations in his heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His malice may be concealed by deception, but his wickedness will be exposed in the assembly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever digs a pit shall fall into it. Whoever rolls a stone, it will come back on him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A lying tongue hates those it hurts; and a flattering mouth works ruin.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t boast about tomorrow; for you don’t know what a day may bring.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let another man praise you, and not your own mouth; a stranger, and not your own lips.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A stone is heavy, and sand is a burden; but a fool’s provocation is heavier than both.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Wrath is cruel, and anger is overwhelming; but who is able to stand before jealousy?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Better is open rebuke than hidden love.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Faithful are the wounds of a friend; although the kisses of an enemy are profuse.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A full soul loathes a honeycomb; but to a hungry soul, every bitter thing is sweet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As a bird that wanders from her nest, so is a man who wanders from his home.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Perfume and incense bring joy to the heart; so does earnest counsel from a man’s friend.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t forsake your friend and your father’s friend. Don’t go to your brother’s house in the day of your disaster: better is a neighbor who is near than a distant brother.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Be wise, my son, and bring joy to my heart, then I can answer my tormentor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A prudent man sees danger and takes refuge; but the simple pass on, and suffer for it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Take his garment when he puts up collateral for a stranger. Hold it for a wayward woman!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who blesses his neighbor with a loud voice early in the morning, it will be taken as a curse by him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A continual dropping on a rainy day and a contentious wife are alike:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: restraining her is like restraining the wind, or like grasping oil in his right hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Iron sharpens iron; so a man sharpens his friend’s countenance.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever tends the fig tree shall eat its fruit. He who looks after his master shall be honored.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Like water reflects a face, so a man’s heart reflects the man.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Sheol  and Abaddon are never satisfied; and a man’s eyes are never satisfied.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The crucible is for silver, and the furnace for gold; but man is refined by his praise.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Though you grind a fool in a mortar with a pestle along with grain, yet his foolishness will not be removed from him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Know well the state of your flocks, and pay attention to your herds:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for riches are not forever, nor does the crown endure to all generations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The hay is removed, and the new growth appears, the grasses of the hills are gathered in.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The lambs are for your clothing, and the goats are the price of a field.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There will be plenty of goats’ milk for your food, for your family’s food, and for the nourishment of your servant girls.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The wicked flee when no one pursues; but the righteous are as bold as a lion.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In rebellion, a land has many rulers, but order is maintained by a man of understanding and knowledge.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A needy man who oppresses the poor is like a driving rain which leaves no crops.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who forsake the law praise the wicked; but those who keep the law contend with them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Evil men don’t understand justice; but those who seek Yahweh understand it fully.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Better is the poor who walks in his integrity, than he who is perverse in his ways, and he is rich.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever keeps the law is a wise son; but he who is a companion of gluttons shames his father.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who increases his wealth by excessive interest gathers it for one who has pity on the poor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who turns away his ear from hearing the law, even his prayer is an abomination.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever causes the upright to go astray in an evil way, he will fall into his own trap; but the blameless will inherit good.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The rich man is wise in his own eyes; but the poor who has understanding sees through him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the righteous triumph, there is great glory; but when the wicked rise, men hide themselves.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who conceals his sins doesn’t prosper, but whoever confesses and renounces them finds mercy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Blessed is the man who always fears; but one who hardens his heart falls into trouble.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As a roaring lion or a charging bear, so is a wicked ruler over helpless people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A tyrannical ruler lacks judgment. One who hates ill-gotten gain will have long days.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A man who is tormented by life blood will be a fugitive until death; no one will support him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever walks blamelessly is kept safe; but one with perverse ways will fall suddenly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One who works his land will have an abundance of food; but one who chases fantasies will have his fill of poverty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A faithful man is rich with blessings; but one who is eager to be rich will not go unpunished.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: To show partiality is not good; yet a man will do wrong for a piece of bread.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A stingy man hurries after riches, and doesn’t know that poverty waits for him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One who rebukes a man will afterward find more favor than one who flatters with the tongue.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever robs his father or his mother, and says, “It’s not wrong.” He is a partner with a destroyer.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One who is greedy stirs up strife; but one who trusts in Yahweh will prosper.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One who trusts in himself is a fool; but one who walks in wisdom is kept safe.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One who gives to the poor has no lack; but one who closes his eyes will have many curses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the wicked rise, men hide themselves; but when they perish, the righteous thrive.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who is often rebuked and stiffens his neck will be destroyed suddenly, with no remedy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the righteous thrive, the people rejoice; but when the wicked rule, the people groan.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever loves wisdom brings joy to his father; but a companion of prostitutes squanders his wealth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king by justice makes the land stable, but he who takes bribes tears it down.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A man who flatters his neighbor spreads a net for his feet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: An evil man is snared by his sin, but the righteous can sing and be glad.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The righteous care about justice for the poor. The wicked aren’t concerned about knowledge.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Mockers stir up a city, but wise men turn away anger.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If a wise man goes to court with a foolish man, the fool rages or scoffs, and there is no peace.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The bloodthirsty hate a man of integrity; and they seek the life of the upright.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A fool vents all of his anger, but a wise man brings himself under control.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If a ruler listens to lies, all of his officials are wicked.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The poor man and the oppressor have this in common: Yahweh gives sight to the eyes of both.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king who fairly judges the poor, his throne shall be established forever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The rod of correction gives wisdom, but a child left to himself causes shame to his mother.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the wicked increase, sin increases; but the righteous will see their downfall.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Correct your son, and he will give you peace; yes, he will bring delight to your soul.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Where there is no revelation, the people cast off restraint; but one who keeps the law is blessed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A servant can’t be corrected by words. Though he understands, yet he will not respond.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Do you see a man who is hasty in his words? There is more hope for a fool than for him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who pampers his servant from youth will have him become a son in the end.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: An angry man stirs up strife, and a wrathful man abounds in sin.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A man’s pride brings him low, but one of lowly spirit gains honor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever is an accomplice of a thief is an enemy of his own soul. He takes an oath, but dares not testify.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The fear of man proves to be a snare, but whoever puts his trust in Yahweh is kept safe.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Many seek the ruler’s favor, but a man’s justice comes from Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A dishonest man detests the righteous, and the upright in their ways detest the wicked.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The words of Agur the son of Jakeh; the revelation: the man says to Ithiel, to Ithiel and Ucal:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Surely I am the most ignorant man, and don’t have a man’s understanding.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have not learned wisdom, neither do I have the knowledge of the Holy One.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who has ascended up into heaven, and descended? Who has gathered the wind in his fists? Who has bound the waters in his garment? Who has established all the ends of the earth? What is his name, and what is his son’s name, if you know?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Every word of God is flawless. He is a shield to those who take refuge in him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t you add to his words, lest he reprove you, and you be found a liar.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Two things I have asked of you; don’t deny me before I die:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Remove far from me falsehood and lies. Give me neither poverty nor riches. Feed me with the food that is needful for me;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: lest I be full, deny you, and say, ‘Who is Yahweh?’ or lest I be poor, and steal, and so dishonor the name of my God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Don’t slander a servant to his master, lest he curse you, and you be held guilty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is a generation that curses their father, and doesn’t bless their mother.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is a generation that is pure in their own eyes, yet are not washed from their filthiness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is a generation, oh how lofty are their eyes! Their eyelids are lifted up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is a generation whose teeth are like swords, and their jaws like knives, to devour the poor from the earth, and the needy from among men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “The leach has two daughters: ‘Give, give.’ “There are three things that are never satisfied; four that don’t say, ‘Enough:’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Sheol,  the barren womb; the earth that is not satisfied with water; and the fire that doesn’t say, ‘Enough.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “The eye that mocks at his father, and scorns obedience to his mother: the ravens of the valley shall pick it out, the young eagles shall eat it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “There are three things which are too amazing for me, four which I don’t understand:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The way of an eagle in the air; the way of a serpent on a rock; the way of a ship in the middle of the sea; and the way of a man with a maiden.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “So is the way of an adulterous woman: she eats and wipes her mouth, and says, ‘I have done nothing wrong.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “For three things the earth tremble, and under four, it can’t bear up:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For a servant when he is king; a fool when he is filled with food;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for an unloved woman when she is married; and a servant who is heir to her mistress.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “There are four things which are little on the earth, but they are exceedingly wise:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the ants are not a strong people, yet they provide their food in the summer.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The conies are but a feeble folk, yet make they their houses in the rocks.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The locusts have no king, yet they advance in ranks.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You can catch a lizard with your hands, yet it is in kings’ palaces.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “There are three things which are stately in their march, four which are stately in going:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The lion, which is mightiest among animals, and doesn’t turn away for any;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the greyhound, the male goat also; and the king against whom there is no rising up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If you have done foolishly in lifting up yourself, or if you have thought evil, put your hand over your mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For as the churning of milk produces butter, and the wringing of the nose produces blood; so the forcing of wrath produces strife.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The words of king Lemuel; the revelation which his mother taught him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Oh, my son! Oh, son of my womb! Oh, son of my vows!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t give your strength to women, nor your ways to that which destroys kings.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It is not for kings, Lemuel; it is not for kings to drink wine; nor for princes to say, ‘Where is strong drink?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: lest they drink, and forget the law, and pervert the justice due to anyone who is afflicted.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Give strong drink to him who is ready to perish; and wine to the bitter in soul:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let him drink, and forget his poverty, and remember his misery no more.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Open your mouth for the mute, in the cause of all who are left desolate.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Open your mouth, judge righteously, and serve justice to the poor and needy.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who can find a worthy woman? For her price is far above rubies.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The heart of her husband trusts in her. He shall have no lack of gain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She does him good, and not harm, all the days of her life.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She seeks wool and flax, and works eagerly with her hands.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She is like the merchant ships. She brings her bread from afar.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She rises also while it is yet night, gives food to her household, and portions for her servant girls.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She considers a field, and buys it. With the fruit of her hands, she plants a vineyard.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She arms her waist with strength, and makes her arms strong.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She perceives that her merchandise is profitable. Her lamp doesn’t go out by night.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She lays her hands to the distaff, and her hands hold the spindle.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She opens her arms to the poor; yes, she extends her hands to the needy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She is not afraid of the snow for her household; for all her household are clothed with scarlet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She makes for herself carpets of tapestry. Her clothing is fine linen and purple.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Her husband is respected in the gates, when he sits among the elders of the land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She makes linen garments and sells them, and delivers sashes to the merchant.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Strength and dignity are her clothing. She laughs at the time to come.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She opens her mouth with wisdom. Faithful instruction is on her tongue.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She looks well to the ways of her household, and doesn’t eat the bread of idleness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Her children rise up and call her blessed. Her husband also praises her:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Many women do noble things, but you excel them all.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Charm is deceitful, and beauty is vain; but a woman who fears Yahweh, she shall be praised.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Give her of the fruit of her hands! Let her works praise her in the gates!
